body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Fable builder empirical program (2x2 + funnel-CI)
body.description:
**What changed:** Adopted Fable's empirical program — a qwen-vs-devstral probe, a funnel-as-CI harness, and a {DS,aider}x{devstral,qwen} 2x2 to resolve the harness-gap-vs-model-floor confound.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) qwen3-coder:30b may be FASTER but WORSE at planning — picking it for speed alone would regress plan quality (watch plan-substantive RATE per arm, not just latency). (2) aider headless against a throwaway clone could still touch the LIVE repo if a path leaks (the 124553ee contamination class) — the aider arms are the highest-risk here. (3) funnel-as-CI's sealed set could ossify — the harness overfits the sealed tickets while real tickets drift (watch external-slice vs internal-slice score divergence). (4) An Ollama Cloud arm could burn free-tier budget (DS cost-constraint). (5) The pre-registered decision rule is only grounding if the rule is written BEFORE results exist — a read-then-write leak silently un-grounds the whole experiment.
**Signals to watch:** per-arm scoreboard rows (plan-substantive rate distinct from latency); io_corpus path-ref scan on the aider arms for any live-repo path; internal-vs-external funnel score gap; per-arm dollars; prereg earliest-record timestamps vs results timestamps.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-07-20 or when the 2x2 scoreboard is first filled.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Completion criteria:**
- By 2026-07-20 (or first filled 2x2 scoreboard): each predicted effect annotated occurred / clear / partial.
- Specifically checked: plan-substantive rate distinct from latency; io_corpus scanned for live-repo path refs in aider arms; internal-vs-external funnel score gap noted; per-arm dollars; prereg timestamps precede results.
**Guardrail side-effects to watch (added 2026-07-06):** (6) prior-art gate (T-sorted-prior-art-gate-in-audit-design) could add filing friction or FALSE-TRIGGER on localized bug-fixes — watch AMEND rate on non-mechanism decisions + waive frequency. (7) flag-stamping (T-agentic-loop-flags-in-corpus) could bloat corpus records or mis-classify a non-flag __init__ arg as a flag — watch record size growth + the structural test's arg-exclusion list.
body.intention: I intend that the empirical program's own side-effects (speed-vs-quality tradeoff, contamination risk, eval ossification, budget burn, firewall integrity) are observed rather than assumed. Serves CP3 (there is always a why) applied to our own instrument.
